title: gaultheria gallowayana
intro: Gaultheria gallowayana, also known as Galloway's Gaultheria, is a species of flowering plant in the heath family Ericaceae. It is native to New Zealand and is a shrub-like plant that grows up to 2 meters in height. The plant has been used traditionally in medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to have various health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation, and potentially aiding in wound healing. Gaultheria gallowayana can be prepared for medicinal use through various herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
